element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Defeated via jumping, tail strike, or nut throw
Alternative traversal method in themed level
Multiple damage types; temporary invulnerability after hit
Final obstacle of each themed level; requires multi-hit defeat
Uses magic powers; goal is to stop ice-based takeover

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Linear level progression with themed environments; no time pressure allows player-paced tension management

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden coins and health items throughout level exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics (jumping, nut collection, basic enemies) in safe contexts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Thematic level variety including water stage; increased enemy complexity and environmental hazards
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalating boss difficulty culminating in final Mr. Blizzard encounter with magic-based attacks
notable_spikes: Boss encounters (multi-hit requirement vs. one-hit regular enemies)
Final antagonist Mr. Blizzard (ice magic powers)

notable_level_patterns.description: Six distinct themed levels, each with environmental characteristics; removed water stage later restored as GBA bonus
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Water stage (removed from original, bonus in GBA)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each themed level concludes with a boss encounter; boss defeats require multiple hits vs. one-hit regular enemies
Coins found throughout levels determine completion bonus; encourages exploration and thoroughness
